title: Chiapasia nelsonii
taxonomy: Chiapasia nelsonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the regions of Chiapas in Mexico. Within the plant kingdom, Chiapasia nelsonii is classified as a member of the Eudicots, a large group of flowering plants. This classification is further divided into the clade of Core eudicots. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is also classified as a member of the clade of Campanulids.

intro: Chiapasia nelsonii, also known as Nelson's Chiapas Rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
